close_reason: Same issue as .6 — requires adding constructors first. Deferred to the same refactor PR.
description: Every public backend struct is missing #[non_exhaustive]: AwsKmsBackend, AwsSmBackend, AwsSsmBackend, AzureKvBackend, BitwardenBackend, DopplerBackend, EnvBackend, FileBackend, GcpSmBackend, VaultBackend, K8sBackend, KeyringBackend, LocalSigningBackend, Pkcs11Backend, SystemdCredsBackend, WolfHsmBackend, DesktopKeyringBackend, CachingStore. None of these structs currently expose public fields, so callers cannot use struct literal syntax anyway — but without #[non_exhaustive], any future addition of a public field (e.g. for diagnostics) is a SemVer-breaking change. More importantly, without the attribute, pattern matching on these structs in exhaustive match arms would need to change if fields are ever added. Consistent use of #[non_exhaustive] signals to callers that the struct is an opaque handle and reserves the right to add fields in minor versions. Fix: add #[non_exhaustive] to all backend structs.
title: All public backend structs missing #[non_exhaustive]
